questions.text: What was the first question that came to the king's mind?
options: What is the right time to begin something?
Who are the right people to listen to?
What is the most important thing to do?
questions.explanation: The king believed that if he knew the right time for every action, he would never fail.

questions.text: The king promised a large sum of money to anyone who could:
questions.explanation: He sent messengers throughout his kingdom promising a reward for the correct answers.

questions.text: To know the right time for every action, some wise men suggested the king should have a:
questions.explanation: Some believed a single man couldn't decide the right time, so a council was needed.

questions.text: Others said that only ________ could look into the future and tell the right time.
questions.explanation: Since some things might be urgent, only magicians were thought to know the future.

questions.text: In answer to the second question, some wise men said the most important people were:
questions.explanation: Different people gave different answers, mentioning councillors, priests, doctors, or soldiers.

questions.text: The king decided to seek the advice of a certain ________ widely known for his wisdom.
questions.explanation: The hermit lived in a wood which he never left and saw only simple people.

questions.text: When the king went to see the hermit, he put on:
questions.explanation: The hermit saw only common folk, so the king disguised himself as a simple person.

questions.text: What was the hermit doing when the king arrived?
questions.explanation: The hermit was digging the ground in front of his hut for seed beds.

questions.text: How did the hermit respond when the king first asked his questions?
options: He told the king to go away
questions.explanation: The hermit continued digging and did not speak to the king initially.

questions.text: The king offered to help the hermit because the hermit looked:
questions.explanation: The hermit was old and weak, and each time he struck the ground, he breathed heavily.

questions.text: Who came running out of the wood while the king was with the hermit?
questions.explanation: A man with his hands pressed against his stomach came running towards them.

questions.text: What had happened to the bearded man?
options: He was bitten by a snake
He was wounded by the king's bodyguards
questions.explanation: He was hiding to ambush the king, but the bodyguards recognized and wounded him.

questions.text: How did the king help the wounded man?
options: He bandaged the wound with his handkerchief
questions.explanation: The king washed and bandaged the man's stomach wound until the bleeding stopped.

questions.text: Why did the bearded man want to kill the king?
options: To take revenge for his brother's death and property
questions.explanation: The king had put the man's brother to death and seized his property.

questions.text: What did the man promise the king after being saved?
options: To serve him as his most faithful servant
questions.explanation: Grateful for his life, the man asked for forgiveness and pledged his loyalty.

questions.text: According to the hermit, what is the only time that is important?
questions.explanation: The most important time is 'Now', because it is the only time we have any power to act.

questions.text: Who is the most necessary person, according to the hermit's final answer?
options: The person you are with at a particular moment
questions.explanation: No one knows what will happen in the future, so the person you are with 'now' is the most important.

questions.text: What is the most important business/thing to do?
options: To do good to the person you are with
questions.explanation: The hermit explained that humans were sent into this life for the purpose of doing good.

questions.text: If the king had not pitied the hermit's weakness and dug the beds, what would have happened?
options: The man would have attacked him on his way back
He would have found the answers earlier
questions.explanation: The hermit pointed out that the king's act of digging saved his life from the ambush.

questions.text: The story 'Three Questions' is written by:
questions.explanation: Leo Tolstoy is a famous Russian author known for this philosophical story.

questions.text: The word 'beds' in the context of the story means:
options: Small patches of ground for plants
questions.explanation: The king helped the hermit dig seed-beds for planting.

questions.text: The king's messengers were sent throughout the kingdom to:
options: Announce the reward for the answers
questions.explanation: They were used to publicize the king's need for wise advice.

questions.text: When the king reached the hermit's hut, he left his ________ with his bodyguards.
questions.explanation: He dismounted and walked the remaining distance alone to appear humble.

questions.text: The hermit was ________ while he spoke to the king for the first time.
questions.explanation: He was weak and old, and the work of digging was physically taxing.

questions.text: Why did the king forgive the bearded man?
options: Because he had made peace with his enemy so easily
Because the hermit told him to
questions.explanation: The king was happy to have gained a friend and resolved a conflict.

questions.text: What does the word 'fainted' mean in the story?
questions.explanation: The wounded man lost consciousness and fell to the ground due to pain and blood loss.

questions.text: The king repeated his questions after digging ________.
questions.explanation: After digging two beds, the king stopped to see if the hermit would answer him.

questions.text: The tone of the story is primarily:
questions.explanation: The story aims to teach a lesson about living in the present and doing good.

questions.text: What did the king use to wash the man's wound?
questions.explanation: He used fresh water to clean the injury as best as he could in the woods.

questions.text: The bearded man was hiding in an ________ to wait for the king.
questions.explanation: He intended to surprise and kill the king on his way home.

questions.text: The king was not satisfied with the answers of the wise men because:
options: They were all different and confusing
questions.explanation: The variety of conflicting answers provided no clear guidance to the king.

questions.text: A 'Council' is a group of people chosen to:
questions.explanation: The king considered forming a council to help him decide the right time for everything.

questions.text: The king spent the night ________ at the hermit's hut.
questions.explanation: He was so tired from the day's work and walk that he slept on the threshold/floor.

questions.text: When did the king decide to ask the questions one last time?
options: In the middle of the night
questions.explanation: Before departing, he made one final attempt to get answers from the hermit.

questions.text: The hermit was sowing seeds in the beds that had been dug ________.
questions.explanation: When the king approached him the next morning, the hermit was busy with the beds they had dug.

questions.text: The hermit told the king that he had ________ been answered.
questions.explanation: The hermit used the events of the previous day to show the king the answers in practice.

questions.text: Which of these is a synonym for 'Hermit'?
questions.explanation: A hermit is someone who lives a solitary and often simple life.

questions.text: What does the king's willingness to dig for the hermit show about his character?
options: He was forced to do it
questions.explanation: It demonstrated that he was down-to-earth and compassionate towards others.

questions.text: The bearded man's initial intention was to take ________ from the king.
questions.explanation: Revenge was the primary motivator for his planned attack.

questions.text: The moral of the story is that life's purpose is to:
options: Serve others and do good in the present
Find all the answers in books
questions.explanation: The story concludes that the only reason humans are sent to this earth is to do good for others.
